subject: The Artisan of Glimmith rule inventory

implementationStatusLegend.ready: Mechanics are verified enough to implement now.
implementationStatusLegend.experimental: Mechanics are likely, but unresolved edge cases remain.
implementationStatusLegend.blocked: Exact semantics are not verified enough for faithful solving.

implementationPolicy.shapeComparison: Support configurable rotation and reflection equivalence.
implementationPolicy.relationClues: Model relation clues as constraints between two regions, not as simple edge states only.

rules.meaning: Every region must contain exactly the configured number of cells.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Use a 2 by 3 board where only two triomino regions satisfy precision = 3.

rules.meaning: Every region must match one of the displayed allowed shapes.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Allow only a straight triomino in the bank and reject an otherwise valid L-triomino region.
openQuestions: Confirm whether bank entries can be reused unlimited times or whether some puzzles limit each listed shape to one use.

rules.meaning: Every region must contain exactly one of each symbol type used by the rule.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: With symbols A, A, B, B, reject any candidate region that lacks one A and one B.
openQuestions: Confirm behavior when multiple symbol families appear in one puzzle.

rules.meaning: The two regions touching the clue must have the same shape.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Place a Gemini marker between a straight triomino candidate and an L-triomino candidate and reject the mixed assignment.
openQuestions: Confirm whether reflections are always accepted or controlled by per-puzzle shape equivalence.

rules.meaning: The two regions touching the clue must have different shapes.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Place a Delta marker between two domino regions and reject assignments where both are the same shape class.
openQuestions: Confirm whether reflections are always accepted or controlled by per-puzzle shape equivalence.

rules.meaning: A region containing a shape clue must have that clue's shape; early puzzles may imply monominoes without showing them.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: A region containing an L-triomino clue should reject straight-triomino candidates.
openQuestions: Confirm behavior when multiple shape clues land in the same region.

rules.meaning: Neighboring regions must have different shapes.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject two edge-adjacent regions with the same tetromino while allowing non-adjacent duplicates.
openQuestions: Confirm whether corner-only contact counts as neighboring.

rules.meaning: A number inside a region indicates the number of cells in that region.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject candidates containing a number 4 clue unless their area is exactly 4.
openQuestions: Confirm behavior for multiple area numbers inside one region.

rules.meaning: A clue prescribes a local border pattern around a clue cell, with variants such as straight, corner, end, dot, or cross.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject a partition around a cross-style clue unless all four required adjacent walls are present.
openQuestions: Capture exact icon taxonomy and orientation rules from the in-game editor or tutorial.

rules.meaning: Regions in the puzzle, or in a marked group, must share the same shape.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject a three-region solution where two regions are L triominoes and the third is a straight triomino.
openQuestions: Confirm whether Match can apply to subsets/groups or always applies to every region.

rules.meaning: Regions must have distinct shapes under the active shape-equivalence settings.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject a solution where two regions are the same pentomino shape.
openQuestions: Confirm whether distinctness is global across all sizes or grouped by area/region class.

rules.meaning: Every region's area must fall within an inclusive minimum and maximum range.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: With range 2..3, reject monomino and tetromino candidates.
openQuestions: Verify inclusivity and whether ranges are always global.

rules.meaning: Each region appears to contain exactly one clue or symbol, but exact wording still needs verification.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject a candidate region containing zero counted clues or two counted clues if the rule is exactly-one.
openQuestions: Verify exactly one vs at most one.
Verify whether all clue types count or only Solitude symbols.

rules.meaning: Neighboring regions must have different areas.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject adjacent regions of areas 3 and 3 while allowing 3 and 4.
openQuestions: Confirm whether same-size non-adjacent regions may repeat.

rules.meaning: Regions must be rectangular boxes.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Keep a 2 by 3 rectangle candidate and reject a bent six-cell candidate.
openQuestions: Verify whether 1 by N lines and monominoes count as Boxy.

rules.meaning: Regions must not be rectangular boxes.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject a 2 by 2 square and allow an L-tetromino.
openQuestions: Verify exact rectangle definition and monomino behavior.

rules.meaning: Region borders may not create a four-line meeting at a grid vertex; sources also describe this as regions not cornering one another.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject four regions meeting around one interior vertex.
openQuestions: Confirm if the rule is exactly no degree-4 boundary vertex or a broader no-corner-touching rule.

rules.meaning: Region borders may not create a three-line meeting at a grid vertex, making boundaries loop-like rather than branching.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject a partition with a T-junction boundary vertex.
openQuestions: Confirm whether degree-1 endpoints are also restricted.
Confirm whether outer border edges count.
Confirm interaction with Bricky.

rules.meaning: An oriented inequality clue between adjacent regions orders their areas; the narrow tip indicates the smaller side.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Reject a `>` assignment where the left region area is less than or equal to the right region area.
openQuestions: Verify exact editor icon orientation.
Verify whether equality has any separate clue.

rules.meaning: A numeric clue between adjacent regions gives the absolute difference between their areas; zero means same area, not necessarily same shape.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: With difference 2, allow adjacent areas 3 and 5 and reject 3 and 4.
openQuestions: Confirm whether the clue can appear on non-edge contacts.

rules.meaning: Confirmed as a window/rule name, but exact mechanics were not found in text sources. It may be a clue-cell visibility or path/flow mechanic.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: Deferred until rule text is captured; likely use a small clue-cell visibility board.
openQuestions: Determine exact clue meaning.
Determine whether it counts cells, regions, symbols, or sight lines.
Determine whether directions are orthogonal only.

rules.meaning: A compass clue appears to count cells of its own region in the eight directional sectors around the clue.
rules.testPuzzleIdea: With an east count of 2, accept only candidates with exactly two region cells in the clue's east sector.
openQuestions: Verify sector boundaries.
Verify whether the clue cell itself counts.
Verify how blank directions are represented.
